refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a real-time communication protocol optimized for low latency and reliability
Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Use modular communication interface layers to isolate network variability from control logic
Implement end-to-end latency monitoring and adaptive quality of service management
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP isolates teleoperation communication as a dedicated channel with prioritized data flow, minimizing coupling with other system functions and supporting latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Prioritization mechanisms do not starve other critical system communications
Local predictive control is not selected, so latency compensation depends solely on communication channel performance
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding a complementary DP for local predictive control algorithms to enhance robustness against occasional latency spikes and improve safety margins.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation commands and feedback communication

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the communication channel for teleoperation commands and feedback data flow

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion and low cohesion due to encompassing many unrelated functions.
Vehicle module is focused on physical vehicle systems; teleoperation communication is peripheral, causing high diffusion.
Highly cohesive for the DP as it directly manages teleoperation commands and feedback; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts, not communication channels; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing the dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback, minimizing diffusion and coupling.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
If teleoperation commands require integration with safety services, cross-module coordination may increase complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow, which is essential to achieve the required end-to-end latency for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
If teleoperation commands require integration with safety services, cross-module coordination may increase complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating a well-defined and cohesive implementation boundary for the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion and lower containment, supporting the choice of Teleoperation as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) of the Teleoperation module's communication channel under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation module is clear and aligns well with the FR, minimizing ambiguity in responsibility and facilitating verification.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface stability requirements and performance expectations for the Network Link to mitigate dependency risks.
Define clear coordination protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules if integration is necessary to reduce complexity and diffusion.
Consider monitoring and logging mechanisms within the Teleoperation module to support latency verification and troubleshooting.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The Teleoperation module demonstrates high containment and low diffusion, indicating a well-defined implementation boundary. The DP directly addresses the FR with a dedicated communication channel ensuring required latency. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is adequate and further sub-FR derivation is not necessary at this stage. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and coordination protocols rather than structural decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface stability and performance expectations for the Network Link, define coordination protocols with Safety Services, and implement monitoring/logging mechanisms to support latency verification and troubleshooting.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module's communication channel can meet the latency requirement under agreed network conditions.
Network Link interface stability and performance can be sufficiently controlled through documentation and protocols.
Cross-module coordination complexity can be managed via defined protocols.
open_risks: Dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
Potential complexity from integration with Safety Services modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under operational network conditions.
Documentation of interface stability and performance requirements.
Defined coordination protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
Monitoring and logging data supporting latency verification.
